UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user's profile and personal preferences for outfit generation.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

email.description: The user's email address, primarily for communication and identification, not for authentication.

name.description: The user's display name or nickname.

gender.description: User's self-identified gender or general style orientation (e.g., 'male', 'female', 'unisex').

occasionPreference.description: User's preferred occasion for outfit suggestions (e.g., 'college', 'work', 'party').

budgetRangePreference.description: User's preferred budget range for outfits (e.g., 'Under ₹1000', '₹1000–₹3000', 'Premium').

preferredColors.description: An array of preferred colors for outfits.

fitType.description: User's preferred fit type for clothing (e.g., 'slim', 'oversized', 'regular').

location.description: User's geographic location, used to tailor brand and product suggestions (e.g., 'India').

isGuest.description: Indicates if the user is currently interacting in guest mode without a full account.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was last updated.

OutfitRequest.title: OutfitRequest
OutfitRequest.description: Stores the parameters for a user's specific outfit generation request.

id.description: Unique identifier for the OutfitRequest entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who made the request. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N OutfitRequest)

heightCm.description: Height in centimeters specified for this particular outfit request.

weightKg.description: Weight in kilograms specified for this particular outfit request.

gender.description: Gender or style preference specified for this particular outfit request.

stylePreference.description: Style preference specified for this particular outfit request (e.g., 'Streetwear', 'Minimal', 'Desi').

occasion.description: Occasion for which the outfit is requested (e.g., 'college', 'work', 'party').

budgetRange.description: Budget range for the requested outfit (e.g., 'Under ₹1000', '₹1000–₹3000', 'Premium').

location.description: Location context for the outfit request, used for localized brand suggestions (e.g., 'India').

timestamp.description: Timestamp when the outfit request was made.

GeneratedOutfit.title: GeneratedOutfit
GeneratedOutfit.description: Stores the details of an AI-generated outfit, including image, items, and shopping links.

id.description: Unique identifier for the GeneratedOutfit entity.

requestId.description: Reference to the OutfitRequest that generated this outfit. (Relationship: OutfitRequest 1:N GeneratedOutfit)

name.description: A descriptive name for the outfit (e.g., 'Street Casual', 'Formal Evening').

type.description: The category or style type of the generated outfit (e.g., 'Budget fit', 'Trendy fit', 'Premium fit').

imageUrl.description: URL to the AI-generated full outfit visual image.

styleTip.description: A brief styling tip or recommendation for the outfit.

totalPrice.description: The estimated total price for all items in the outfit, including currency symbol (e.g., '₹2797').

items.description: An array of individual clothing items and accessories that constitute the outfit.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the outfit was generated.

OutfitItem.title: OutfitItem
OutfitItem.description: Represents a single clothing item or accessory within a generated outfit. This is typically embedded within a GeneratedOutfit entity.

name.description: A descriptive name for the item (e.g., 'Oversized Black T-shirt').

price.description: The estimated price of the individual item, including currency symbol (e.g., '₹499').

links.description: An array of affiliate links for the item across various platforms.

AffiliateLink.title: AffiliateLink
AffiliateLink.description: Represents a single affiliate link for an outfit item.

platform.description: The e-commerce platform for this link (e.g., 'amazon', 'myntra', 'ajio', 'flipkart', 'meesho', 'nykaaFashion', 'tataCliq', 'hM', 'zara').

url.description: The affiliate URL for the product on this platform.

ClickEvent.title: ClickEvent
ClickEvent.description: Tracks user clicks on affiliate links for monetization and analytics.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ClickEvent entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who performed the click. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N ClickEvent)

outfitId.description: Reference to the GeneratedOutfit from which the item was clicked. (Relationship: GeneratedOutfit 1:N ClickEvent)

itemType.description: The type of the item that was clicked (e.g., 'T-shirt', 'Shoes').

platform.description: The e-commerce platform where the link was clicked (e.g., 'amazon', 'myntra').

productLink.description: The actual affiliate URL that was clicked.

timestamp.description: Timestamp when the click event occurred.

definition.description: Stores user profiles and preferences. Each document represents a single user and is secured by the user's UID. The `id` field within the document will be identical to `userId` in the path for consistency. This ensures private data ownership.

params.name: userId
params.description: The Firebase Authentication UID of the user.

definition.description: Stores individual outfit generation requests made by a user. Ownership is path-based. Includes denormalized `userId` field for authorization independence and simplified security rules.

params.name: userId
params.description: The Firebase Authentication UID of the user who made the request.

params.name: requestId
params.description: The unique identifier for an outfit generation request.

definition.description: Stores AI-generated outfits, including embedded `OutfitItem`s and `AffiliateLink`s. Each document includes the denormalized `userId` from the parent user for authorization independence, preventing `get()` calls in security rules. The `requestId` from the parent is also in the path for clear context.

params.name: userId
params.description: The Firebase Authentication UID of the user who owns this generated outfit.

params.name: requestId
params.description: The unique identifier of the outfit request that generated this outfit.

params.name: outfitId
params.description: The unique identifier for this specific generated outfit.

definition.description: Records user clicks on affiliate links for analytics and monetization tracking. The `userId` field is explicitly denormalized within the document for authorization independence, allowing direct ownership checks during creation and QAP-compliant listing.

params.name: clickId
params.description: The unique identifier for a click event.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for VYXEN is designed with core principles of Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and consistent Access Modeling to ensure security, scalability, and debuggability. All user-specific data is nested under `/users/{userId}/...`, leveraging path-based ownership for simple and robust security rules. Each document in subcollections (e.g., `outfitRequests`, `generatedOutfits`) explicitly includes the `userId` field. This critical denormalization ensures Authorization Independence by eliminating the need for `get()` calls to parent documents in security rules. For example, a `GeneratedOutfit` document can verify `request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId` directly, preventing complex and insecure hierarchical authorization checks and enabling atomic operations. This design also inherently supports QAPs (Query-Authenticated Paths) as `list` operations for user-owned subcollections (e.g., `/users/{userId}/outfitRequests`, `/users/{userId}/outfitRequests/{requestId}/generatedOutfits`) can directly leverage the `{userId}` wildcard in the path, ensuring that only data belonging to `request.auth.uid` is retrieved, thus preventing rules from acting as filters. The `clicks` collection is segregated to handle analytical data with distinct access requirements (creatable by users, readable potentially by admins or via specific user queries).

Specifically:

*   **UserProfile (`/users/{userId}`):** This is the root for all user-specific data. The `userId` in the path matches `request.auth.uid`, establishing clear ownership. The `id` field within the document will also store this `userId` for consistency and direct reference. This ensures only the authenticated user can access their profile.
*   **OutfitRequest (`/users/{userId}/outfitRequests/{requestId}`):** Nested under the user, this collection stores individual outfit generation requests. The `userId` is present in the path and also explicitly stored in the document's `userId` field. This denormalization means rules can verify `request.auth.uid == request.resource.data.userId` without needing to retrieve the parent `/users/{userId}` document. A `list` query on `/users/{request.auth.uid}/outfitRequests` will inherently be secure as the path itself scopes the query to the authenticated user.
*   **GeneratedOutfit (`/users/{userId}/outfitRequests/{requestId}/generatedOutfits/{outfitId}`):** These are generated outfits, embedded with `OutfitItem` and `AffiliateLink` details. Crucially, each `GeneratedOutfit` document *must* contain the `userId` field, denormalized from the parent user. This allows security rules to evaluate `request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId` directly on the `GeneratedOutfit` document itself, making it authorization independent. This structure also supports QAPs for listing outfits for a specific request or user.
*   **ClickEvent (`/clicks/{clickId}`):** This collection is a top-level collection for tracking all user clicks. Each `ClickEvent` document includes the `userId` of the user who performed the click. This allows for independent creation by any authenticated user (including guests) with rules like `allow create: if request.auth.uid != null && request.auth.uid == request.resource.data.userId;`. Listing or querying for specific user clicks is QAP-compliant as the `userId` field can be used in queries and checked by rules (`allow list: if request.auth.uid != null && request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId;`).

This robust structure minimizes rule complexity, enhances security by design, and ensures atomic operations are not hampered by authorization dependencies.
